tasks.title: Add callbackUrl param support to web signin page (server-side redirect)
tasks.description: In apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/auth/signin.astro: read the ?callbackUrl query param in server-side Astro code. After a successful sign-in, if callbackUrl is present and passes the allowlist check (T-002), redirect to callbackUrl instead of the default returnUrl destination. If callbackUrl is absent or fails allowlist, use existing returnUrl logic unchanged. The redirect must happen in server-side Astro.redirect() — NOT in client JS. Acceptance: signin with ?callbackUrl=https://admin.hospeda.com.ar redirects there post-signin; signin without ?callbackUrl behaves identically to today.

tasks.title: Implement callbackUrl allowlist validator (server-side, no open-redirect)
tasks.description: Create a pure function validateCallbackUrl(url: string, env: { siteUrl: string; adminUrl: string; nodeEnv: string }): string | null in apps/web/src/lib/auth-callback.ts (or middleware-helpers.ts). Returns the URL if allowed, null otherwise. Allowlist rules: (1) hospeda.com.ar and any subdomain (regex ^https?://([a-z0-9-]+\.)?hospeda\.com\.ar); (2) HOSPEDA_SITE_URL and HOSPEDA_ADMIN_URL exact origins; (3) *.hospeda.local + localhost in development. MUST NOT allow arbitrary domains. Acceptance: unit tests cover allowed/blocked cases; open-redirect with evil.example.com returns null.

tasks.title: Add buildAdminLoginRedirect helper in web middleware-helpers.ts
tasks.description: In apps/web/src/lib/middleware-helpers.ts, add an exported function buildAdminLoginRedirect({ siteUrl, locale, adminUrl }: { siteUrl: string; locale: SupportedLocale; adminUrl: string }): string that returns a URL of the form /{locale}/auth/signin/?callbackUrl={encodeURIComponent(adminUrl)}. This is the URL the admin guard will redirect unauthenticated users to. Note: this function lives in the web app but is also referenced conceptually by the admin guard (which builds the equivalent URL from its env vars — see T-005). Acceptance: unit test covers the URL shape and encoding; existing buildLoginRedirect is unchanged.

tasks.title: Unit tests: callbackUrl allowlist + buildAdminLoginRedirect
tasks.description: Write Vitest unit tests for validateCallbackUrl (T-002) and buildAdminLoginRedirect (T-003). Coverage: (a) allowed origins pass (hospeda.com.ar, admin.hospeda.com.ar, staging.hospeda.com.ar, SITE_URL, ADMIN_URL), (b) blocked origins return null (evil.com, evil.hospeda.com.ar.evil.com, javascript:, //, data:), (c) relative URLs return null, (d) buildAdminLoginRedirect encodes the callbackUrl correctly, (e) existing returnUrl behavior in signin is not regressed. Acceptance: all tests green, coverage ≥90% on the two functions.

tasks.title: Update admin /_authed.tsx redirect-signin to point to web auth URL
tasks.description: In apps/admin/src/routes/_authed.tsx, change the 'redirect-signin' case from `throw redirect({ to: '/auth/signin', search: decision.search })` to an external redirect: build the URL {VITE_SITE_URL}/{locale}/auth/signin?callbackUrl={encodeURIComponent(location.pathname + location.search)} and throw redirect({ href: webSigninUrl }). Use env.VITE_SITE_URL for the web origin. The admin's own env.VITE_ADMIN_URL (or window.location.origin) is the callbackUrl value so the web signin knows where to send the user after auth. Acceptance: unauthenticated request to any admin route redirects to web signin with correct callbackUrl; typecheck clean.

tasks.title: Delete apps/admin/src/routes/auth/signup.tsx
tasks.description: Remove the logged-out host signup page from admin. This closes BETA-57: the OAuth signup path that created role=USER instead of HOST no longer exists. Before deleting: check for any import references to this file in the admin app (routes manifest, auth.tsx, etc.) and remove them. Acceptance: file is gone, typecheck passes, no dangling imports, routes tree does not 404 on any currently-linked page (the signup link from signin.tsx is also going away in T-007).

tasks.title: Delete apps/admin/src/routes/auth/signin.tsx
tasks.description: Remove the admin-own signin page. This is now replaced by the web auth redirect (T-005). Before deleting: check the header-user.tsx (window.location.href = '/auth/signin') and callback.tsx (router.navigate to /auth/signin) references — update them to redirect to web signin URL or remove if unreachable. Also check auth.tsx (the route layout) for any direct link to /auth/signin and update. Acceptance: file gone, all former references updated, typecheck clean.

tasks.title: Update admin auth/index.tsx + auth/callback.tsx for removed signin/signup routes
tasks.description: Review apps/admin/src/routes/auth/index.tsx: if it redirects to /auth/signin, update the redirect to the web signin URL. If it is a layout, ensure it does not reference the deleted pages. Review apps/admin/src/routes/auth/callback.tsx: after OAuth code exchange, if it redirects to /auth/signin on failure (unauthenticated), update to redirect to web signin URL. On success it redirects to /dashboard — keep that unchanged. Acceptance: auth index and callback handle the missing pages gracefully; typecheck clean.

tasks.title: Extend authed-guard unit tests to cover web-auth redirect shape
tasks.description: In the existing authed-guard.test.ts (or create one if absent), add/extend tests for the 'redirect-signin' case to verify that the decision carries the correct pathname for use by the caller in building the web auth URL. Also add an integration-level test (or snapshot) for the _authed.tsx handler: given authState.isAuthenticated=false, verify the redirect href matches the expected web signin URL pattern (including callbackUrl encoding). Acceptance: tests green, existing guard tests preserved.

tasks.title: [RESOLVED] Confirm permission for staff host-creation (D1 + D2)
tasks.description: RESOLVED 2026-06-02. D1=USER_CREATE (existing permission, YAGNI). D2=modal on the admin users list (lowest friction). Spec §8 updated. T-011/T-012/T-013 are unblocked.

tasks.title: Migrate signup-as-host endpoint from Origin-check to permission-check
tasks.description: In apps/api/src/routes/auth/signup-as-host.ts: remove the Origin header guard and move the endpoint from createPublicRoute to an authenticated route (createProtectedRoute or a dedicated admin route). Gate with PermissionEnum.USER_CREATE (D1 resolved: use USER_CREATE, not HOST_ONBOARD). The session is validated by the route factory middleware — no manual session check needed. Remove the route from apps/api/src/routes/auth/index.ts if the path changes; add it to the correct admin route index. Update the response schema if needed. Acceptance: authenticated user with USER_CREATE can create a host; unauthenticated 401; authenticated without permission 403; typecheck clean.

tasks.title: Add in-panel staff 'Create host account' UI action (admin)
tasks.description: D2 resolved: modal on the admin users list page. Add a 'Create host account' button to the admin users list. The modal/form has name + email + password fields and calls the migrated endpoint (T-011). On success, show a toast and refresh the users list. On error, show the API error message. Requires the user to have USER_CREATE permission (the button is hidden or disabled otherwise). Acceptance: staff can create a host account in-panel; tourists/hosts without USER_CREATE do not see the button; typecheck clean.

tasks.title: Tests: staff host-creation endpoint permission matrix
tasks.description: Write Vitest integration tests for the migrated signup-as-host endpoint (T-011): (a) authenticated user with USER_CREATE → 200 + role=HOST in response, (b) unauthenticated → 401, (c) authenticated without USER_CREATE → 403, (d) missing fields → 400 (Zod validation), (e) duplicate email → appropriate error from Better Auth. Use the project's standard mock-actor auth pattern. Acceptance: all 5 cases covered, tests green.

tasks.title: [RESOLVED] Confirm D3 (host CTA check) and D5 (callbackUrl param name)
tasks.description: RESOLVED 2026-06-02. D3=derive from role=HOST (no extra API call; role=HOST implies ≥1 published accommodation by model). D5=callbackUrl (semantically distinct from same-app returnUrl; consistent with web's existing usage). Spec §8 updated. T-015/T-016 are unblocked.

tasks.title: Wire UserMenu.client.tsx hostMode item to admin URL for role=HOST
tasks.description: In apps/web/src/components/shared/navigation/UserMenu.client.tsx: the 'hostMode' i18n key (es/en/pt) and the STAFF_DISCRIMINATOR_PERMISSION logic already exist. Add a three-state CTA based on /auth/me response: (1) unauthenticated/tourist → href=/{locale}/publicar/, (2) role=HOST → href=env.VITE_ADMIN_URL (or HOSPEDA_ADMIN_URL from PUBLIC_ADMIN_URL), (3) staff with access.apiAdmin → href=env.VITE_ADMIN_URL with 'Panel de administración' label. The 'Modo anfitrión' label is already i18n'd. D3 resolved: derive host state from role===HOST — no extra API call needed. Acceptance: host user sees 'Modo anfitrión' linking to admin; staff sees 'Panel de administración'; tourist sees 'Publicar alojamiento'; typecheck clean.

tasks.title: Extend HostLandingCta + mobile nav CTA for host-mode three-state logic
tasks.description: In apps/web/src/components/sections/HostLandingCta.client.tsx: extend existing unauthenticated/authenticated branches to add the role=HOST case (CTA → admin URL). In apps/web/src/components/shared/navigation/MobileMenuIsland.astro (and desktop nav CTA if applicable): apply the same three-state logic. D3 resolved: role===HOST → admin URL (no extra API call); role=USER or unauthenticated → /{locale}/publicar/. Use PUBLIC_ADMIN_URL (or an existing env var) as the admin URL. Acceptance: all three CTA surfaces adapt correctly per user state; typecheck clean; no regression on existing tourist flow.

tasks.title: [RESOLVED] Confirm dev-local cookie approach (D4)
tasks.description: RESOLVED 2026-06-02. D4=*.hospeda.local + /etc/hosts recipe (option A). T-018 must implement the DEV_COOKIE_DOMAIN env var in apps/api/src/lib/auth.ts and document the /etc/hosts setup in docs/guides/auth-local-dev.md. Spec §8 updated.

tasks.title: Implement dev-local cross-subdomain cookie workaround (*.hospeda.local recipe)
tasks.description: D4 resolved: implement option A (*.hospeda.local + /etc/hosts). (1) In apps/api/src/lib/auth.ts: add DEV_COOKIE_DOMAIN support — if process.env.DEV_COOKIE_DOMAIN is set, use it as crossSubDomainCookies.domain regardless of NODE_ENV. Register DEV_COOKIE_DOMAIN in packages/config/src/env-registry.*.ts as a dev-only, non-secret var. Update apps/api/.env.example. (2) Create docs/guides/auth-local-dev.md with /etc/hosts setup (web.hospeda.local + admin.hospeda.local → 127.0.0.1), DEV_COOKIE_DOMAIN config, and verification steps. Acceptance: developer can sign in at http://web.hospeda.local:4321 and navigate to http://admin.hospeda.local:3000 with the same session cookie; typecheck clean.

tasks.title: Full test suite pass + typecheck clean across all changed packages
tasks.description: Run the full test suite for all changed packages: apps/web (vitest), apps/admin (vitest), apps/api (vitest), packages/schemas. Fix any regressions introduced by SPEC-182 changes. Confirm pnpm typecheck passes with zero errors on all changed packages. Run pnpm lint / biome check — fix any lint issues. Acceptance: all tests green, typecheck clean, lint clean across web/admin/api/schemas.

tasks.title: Write docs/guides/auth-architecture.md — unified auth reference
tasks.description: Create docs/guides/auth-architecture.md documenting: (1) the unified auth model (web is the single auth surface, admin consumes the shared cookie), (2) the role matrix table (unauthenticated / tourist / host / staff / change-password), (3) cross-subdomain cookie setup (production: Better Auth crossSubDomainCookies; dev-local: T-018 workaround), (4) how HOST role is earned (action-triggered on first publish, not declared at signup), (5) staff host-creation back-office action, (6) the cross-app coupling risk (admin auth depends on web — runbook note), (7) references to SPEC-169 (owner-scoping) and SPEC-182. Acceptance: doc is complete, accurate, and covers all operator and developer scenarios.

tasks.title: [OWNER ACTION] Manual smoke — web→admin round-trip per role
tasks.description: OPERATOR ONLY — not automatable in CI. Using a staging or local environment (with dev-local cookie if D4=A), verify the round-trip for each role: (1) UNAUTH: visit admin → lands on web signin with ?callbackUrl=admin-url → sign in → lands on admin dashboard. (2) TOURIST: admin guard → tourist funnel (/{locale}/publicar/?from=admin). (3) HOST: web signin → admin → 'Modo anfitrión' badge + owner-scoped accommodations. (4) STAFF: web signin → admin → full access. (5) STAFF creates host account via in-panel action → new host can sign in on web → access admin with 'Modo anfitrión'. Acceptance: all 5 flows verified; no session cookie issues; BETA-52 and BETA-57 confirmed closed.

tasks.title: Flip spec + task indexes to completed and open PR
tasks.description: After T-021 passes: (1) Update .qtm/specs/index.json: SPEC-182 status → completed, archived: true, archivedAt: <ISO date>. (2) Update .qtm/tasks/index.json: SPEC-182 epic status → completed, progress → 22/22, archived: true. (3) Update spec.md frontmatter: status → completed. (4) Open PR to staging targeting branch spec/SPEC-182-unified-auth-host-access. Validate JSON parses (python3 -c 'import json; json.load(open(...))') before committing. Stage and commit each index file individually (never git add -A). Acceptance: both index.json files are valid JSON, spec.md updated, PR open to staging, CI green.
